generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building automation system for adaptive lighting and energy optimization policies derived from reinforcement learning (BOREALIS project)"
]

instance LightingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting system control component for adaptive lighting based on occupancy and environmental conditions"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy optimization controller implementing reinforcement learning policies for building operations"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy occupancy sensors that monitor room occupancy patterns in the LEED Platinum academic building"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataStream
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental temperature sensors embedded in building infrastructure for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy usage monitors tracking real-time power consumption across building systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataStream
]

instance EnvironmentalMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive environmental monitors measuring multiple parameters (humidity, CO2, air quality) in building spaces"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalParametersDataStream
]

instance LightingPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting performance sensors tracking energy efficiency and illumination quality metrics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingPerformanceDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OccupancyDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalParametersDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalMonitor
]

instance LightingPerformanceDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingPerformanceSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D spatial and functional representations of building components (walls, rooms) with embedded metadata from BIM models"
]

instance RuntimeDataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Extracted runtime data model from physical sensors via Eclipse Ditto, including time-series data (energy usage, occupancy patterns)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Defines lighting control rules and energy optimization policies defined by Building Control Engineers in BIM tools"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringPipeline
]

instance BIMMetadataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Metadata model containing sensor placements, device IDs, and tenant identifiers derived from BIM annotations"
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data processed from physical sensors via Eclipse Ditto for bidirectional synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataStream, TemperatureDataStream, EnergyConsumptionDataStream, EnvironmentalParametersDataStream, LightingPerformanceDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy data analysis including occupancy patterns and energy waste metrics for operational decision making"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeMonitoringData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerformanceAnalyticsService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance AutodeskRevit : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM platform (Autodesk Revit) used for device modeling and control logic definition in the digital twin framework"
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automates BIM-to-DT transformations using model-to-model/model-to-text techniques for sensor configuration extraction"
    DTDFVocab:enables BIMMetadataModel, ControlLogicModel
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange middleware enabling bidirectional communication between physical sensors and digital twin"
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bidirectional synchronization middleware for real-time data exchange between physical building systems and digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringData, HistoricalPerformanceData
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization platform for immersive building monitoring and simulation"
]

instance StandardizedExtensionPoints : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized interfaces for DT Engineers to integrate custom services (AI analytics, IoT gateways)"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization service using Unreal Engine 5 for real-time building condition monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operational optimization service implementing reinforcement learning for energy-efficient policies (BOREALIS project)"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationsAction
]

instance PerformanceAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy data analysis service tracking occupancy patterns and energy waste for operational decisions"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceMetricsInsight
]

instance InteractiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance modeling service leveraging BIM data for clash detection and simulation analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultsAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Extension service platform for deploying domain-specific plugins (AI-driven predictive maintenance, IoT analytics)"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomPluginAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D spatial representation of building conditions including real-time sensor readings and immersive visualization capabilities"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated energy optimization policies derived from reinforcement learning for adaptive building control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive performance metrics including occupancy patterns and energy waste analysis for operational decision making"
]

instance SimulationResultsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Simulation outputs generated from BIM data for performance modeling and clash detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomPluginAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Domain-specific service actions enabled through standardized extension points for custom implementations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment and Components
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BuildingControlSystem, LightingControlSystem, EnergyOptimizationSystem,
        OccupancySensor, TemperatureSensor, EnergyConsumptionSensor, EnvironmentalMonitor, LightingPerformanceSensor
]

instance LEEDPlatinumBuilding : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AcademicBuildingEnvironment,
        BIMStructuralModel, RuntimeDataModel, ControlLogicModel, BIMMetadataModel
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals and optimization commands generated by the DT (e.g., adaptive lighting adjustments, energy-efficient scheduling) are sent back to physical actuators through **Eclipse Hono** middleware, ensuring bidirectional synchronization. The framework leverages BIM-enriched device configurations to automate the mapping of virtual commands to physical endpoints, reducing manual intervention."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** time scale, with synchronization rates determined by sensor data acquisition frequencies (e.g., 1-second intervals for lighting control, 5-minute intervals for energy analytics). The framework ensures low-latency bidirectional communication via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**, enabling adaptive control loops (e.g., **BOREALIS** reinforcement learning updates) to respond dynamically to operational changes."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented ecosystem** with:
  1. A **centralized DT framework** acting as the core hub for bidirectional communication (via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**).
  2. **Modular sub-twins** for hierarchical decomposition (e.g., room-level vs. building-wide services).
  3. **Interoperability layers** ensuring compatibility with external systems (e.g., BIM tools, IoT platforms) via standardized APIs and ontologies.
  4. **Real-time synchronization** between physical assets and virtual models, enabling adaptive control and monitoring workflows."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans the entire building life cycle, covering:
  1. **Design phase**: Enriched BIM models with device semantics (e.g., lighting control logic) for automated DT generation.
  2. **Construction phase**: Validation of BIM-to-DT transformations to ensure physical- digital alignment.
  3. **Operation phase**: Real-time monitoring and adaptive control (e.g., **BOREALIS** reinforcement learning updates) using post-occupancy sensor data.
  The framework ensures compatibility with existing AECO standards (e.g., BIM) and supports evolution through modular service integration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  1. **Semantic alignment**: BIM models are enriched with runtime semantics (e.g., sensor placements, control logic) to minimize **semantic drift** (e.g., Abbasi et al.’s definition).
  2. **Validation metrics**: The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE)** metric quantifies the interoperability and architectural soundness of the DT.
  3. **Verification**: Automated checks during BIM-to-DT transformations to detect inconsistencies (e.g., missing sensor mappings).
  4. **Uncertainty handling**: Runtime data is processed with confidence thresholds (e.g., sensor noise filtering) to maintain model validity."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Technical connections are established via:
  1. **Network protocols**: **MQTT** (via **Eclipse Hono**) for lightweight, real-time data exchange between physical sensors and DT.
  2. **Middleware**: **Eclipse Ditto** for bidirectional synchronization, enabling bidirectional data flows (e.g., sensor readings → DT, DT commands → actuators).
  3. **APIs**: Standardized interfaces for integrating external systems (e.g., BIM tools, IoT gateways) via RESTful or WebSocket protocols."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed as a **scalable, cloud-native service** with:
  1. **Hosting infrastructure**: Distributed across edge and cloud environments for low-latency processing (e.g., sensor data aggregation).
  2. **Runtime artifacts**: Generated from BIM models via MDE pipelines, including:
     - **Service-oriented components** (e.g., lighting control logic, analytics plugins).
     - **Real-time data pipelines** (e.g., **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** middleware).
  3. **Deployment workflow**: Automated via CI/CD pipelines, enabling reproducible DT setups with minimal manual intervention."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration occurs via:
  1. **BIM interoperability**: Seamless exchange with AECO standards (e.g., IFC, COBie) to connect with external systems (e.g., facility management platforms).
  2. **Middleware bridges**: **Eclipse Ditto** enables interoperability with IoT gateways and proprietary BIM tools (e.g., Bentley iTwin.js).
  3. **Standardized APIs**: Extension points allow DT Engineers to integrate third-party services (e.g., cloud analytics, AI models) without disrupting core functionality."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are managed as follows:
  1. **Ownership**: Physical building data (e.g., sensor readings) is owned by the **PT (Physical Twin) owner**, while DT-generated analytics (e.g., energy reports) may be owned by the service provider (DT Engineer).
  2. **Privacy**: Compliance with AECO regulations (e.g., GDPR) is ensured via:
     - Encrypted data transmission (e.g., TLS for **Eclipse Hono**).
     - Anonymization of user-specific data (e.g., occupancy patterns).
     - Role-based access control (e.g., **Facility Managers** vs. **DT Engineers**)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to the following standards:
  1. **AECO-specific**: **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** (e.g., IFC, COBie) for semantic integration.
  2. **Digital Twin**: **Eclipse Ditto** and **Eclipse Hono** for interoperability and real-time connectivity.
  3. **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)**: Industry-standard metamodels (e.g., OMG MOF) for automated transformations.
  4. **AI/ML**: **Reinforcement Learning (BOREALIS project)** for adaptive control policies, aligned with AECO performance standards."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  1. **Cybersecurity**: Encrypted data transmission (e.g., TLS for **Eclipse Hono**), authentication (e.g., OAuth2), and intrusion detection for remote operations.
  2. **Safety mechanisms**: Human-in-the-loop validation for critical control actions (e.g., emergency shutdowns) to prevent accidents.
  3. **Risk mitigation**: Fallback procedures for DT failures (e.g., manual override for facility managers) and redundancy in middleware (e.g., **Eclipse Ditto** failover clusters)."
]
